intro: Cardosanctus, also known as Cardoon thistle or Milk thistle of the Mediterranean, is a type of spiny, thistle-like plant belonging to the Compositae family. The plant has been traditionally used to promote liver health and protect against liver damage, with its health benefits attributed to its ability to stimulate liver regeneration and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Cardosanctus include flavonoids, particularly silymarin, which is known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations made from Cardosanctus include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support liver health and treat conditions such as liver cirrhosis, hepatitis, and jaundice.
